messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Create a small library with one function and a unit test.
Clarifications:
None
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Create a small library with one function and a unit test.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-project",
      "title": "Initialize project repository and environment",
      "description": "Create a project directory, initialize a Git repository, and create a virtual environment or equivalent workspace for dependencies.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A project folder exists with an initialized Git repository and an activated virtual environment or documented environment setup steps."
    },
    {
      "id": "setup-project-metadata",
      "title": "Add project metadata and test dependencies",
      "description": "Add pyproject.toml or setup.cfg (or package.json for JS) and declare the test runner dependency (e.g., pytest). Ensure a requirements file or lockfile is present.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Project metadata file contains package info and test dependency; running the package manager installs dev/test dependencies without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-function",
      "title": "Implement the library function",
      "description": "Create the library file and implement a single, well-documented function with type hints and basic input validation.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "setup-project-metadata"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A source file exists with one exported function that follows docstring/type conventions and passes basic manual invocation for expected inputs."
    },
    {
      "id": "write-unit-test",
      "title": "Write unit test for the function",
      "description": "Create a unit test file that imports the function and contains tests for typical cases, edge cases, and at least one failure case using the chosen test framework.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-function"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A test file exists and includes multiple assertions that comprehensively cover normal and edge behavior of the function."
    },
    {
      "id": "run-tests",
      "title": "Run test suite and fix issues",
      "description": "Execute the test runner, inspect failures, update code or tests as needed, and repeat until all tests pass reliably.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "write-unit-test"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All tests pass consistently on the local environment with exit code 0 from the test runner."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-readme",
      "title": "Add README with usage and test instructions",
      "description": "Create a README.md describing the library purpose, the function signature, usage examples, and instructions to run the unit tests.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-function",
        "write-unit-test"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "README.md contains clear usage example(s) and step-by-step instructions to install dependencies and run the tests."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 6,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Sequential functional decomposition: set up environment, declare metadata, implement code, create tests, validate by running tests, then document."
}
